acceptanceCriteria: Every consumed evidence field is validated for presence and type; schema-required fields that are absent or wrong-typed make the artifact count as malformed instead of defaulting (retryCount, escalationCount, humanCorrectionMinutes, provider, model, usage.modelIdentity/providerCost types, artifactId, routingPlanArtifactId)
A malformed excluded attempt counts as an unknown relative-cost contribution so partial cost is never presented as complete
rigor calibration-proposal verifies each cited report artifact: the file is supplied, hashes to the listed reportHash, and embeds the selected manifest hash
The evaluation-report schema const-binds split discriminators (calibration/evaluationOnly false, holdout/evaluationOnly true) with negative tests
An attempts or routing collection directory escaping containment yields a nonzero malformed count, with collection-directory and file-level symlink tests
The candidate key uses an unambiguous encoding immune to delimiter collision, with a collision test
Coverage gaps closed: accepted-outcome-missing-elapsed test and malformed-excluded-attempt cost test
All checks pass: npm run test:all, npm run bench, claude plugin validate . --strict, git diff --check
The sample report is still produced entirely from checked-in synthetic fixtures
constraints: Remediate exactly the residual findings recorded in consultation_d6e84429-1d3f-468f-ab84-60a7391ab0cf under GH-16-R3; no unrelated changes
Do not delete, weaken, or skip existing tests or configured checks
Existing artifact schemas and consumers outside this change remain accepted unchanged
Configured relative cost remains an abstract comparison weight; never a price, token count, or provider charge
Unknown data stays unknown/null; never inferred
Fixtures stay synthetic, secret-free, without absolute paths
Stop if remediation requires paths outside the allowed list
stopConditions: a required check fails twice without a new hypothesis
